   "source": [
    "# CODTECH Internship — Task 4\n",
    "# Recommendation System\n",
    "\n",
    "**Objective:** Build a recommendation system using **matrix factorization** and **collaborative filtering**, and report recommendation results with evaluation metrics.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Dataset:** **MovieLens 100K** (100,000 ratings, 943 users, 1,682 movies) — the classic benchmark for recommender systems. The notebook downloads it automatically; if the download is unavailable it **falls back to a synthetic ratings dataset with latent structure** so the notebook always runs end-to-end.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Two approaches implemented**\n",
    "1. **Matrix Factorization** built from scratch with SGD (latent user/item factors + biases).\n",
    "2. **Item-based Collaborative Filtering** using cosine similarity.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Deliverable checklist**\n",
    "- [x] Load ratings data (real or synthetic fallback)\n",
    "- [x] Explore sparsity & rating distribution\n",
    "- [x] Matrix Factorization model (RMSE / MAE)\n",
    "- [x] Item-based Collaborative Filtering (RMSE)\n",
    "- [x] **Top-N recommendations** for a user\n",
    "- [x] **Ranking metrics**: Precision@K & Recall@K\n",
    "\n",
    "*Author: CODTECH ML Internship submission*"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "01f57416",
   "metadata": {

    "# ---- Imports ----\n",
    "import io, os, zipfile, urllib.request\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import seaborn as sns\n",
    "from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split\n",
    "from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity\n",
    "from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error\n",
    "\n",
    "sns.set_theme(style=\"whitegrid\")\n",
    "np.random.seed(42)\n",
    "print(\"Libraries imported.\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "c000b83a",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Load the ratings data\n",
    "\n",
    "We try to download **MovieLens 100K**. If that fails (e.g. no internet access to the dataset host), we generate a realistic **synthetic** ratings matrix that has genuine latent structure, so every cell below still works."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,

   "metadata": {

     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-14T04:22:01.650707Z"
    }
   },

     ]
    },

      "\n",

      "Ratings: 7223\n"
     ]
    },
    {

    "def load_movielens_100k():\n",
    "    \"\"\"Return a DataFrame with columns [userId, itemId, rating]; download or synthesize.\"\"\"\n",
    "    url = \"https://files.grouplens.org/datasets/movielens/ml-100k.zip\"\n",
    "    try:\n",
    "        print(\"Attempting to download MovieLens 100K ...\")\n",
    "        raw = urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=20).read()\n",
    "        with zipfile.ZipFile(io.BytesIO(raw)) as z:\n",
    "            with z.open(\"ml-100k/u.data\") as f:\n",
    "                df = pd.read_csv(f, sep=\"\\t\",\n",
    "                                 names=[\"userId\", \"itemId\", \"rating\", \"timestamp\"])\n",
    "        df = df.drop(columns=\"timestamp\")\n",
    "        print(\"Loaded REAL MovieLens 100K.\")\n",
    "        return df, \"MovieLens 100K\"\n",
    "    except Exception as e:\n",
    "        print(\"Download failed (\", type(e).__name__, \") -> using synthetic fallback.\")\n",
    "        # ---- Synthetic ratings with latent structure ----\n",
    "        n_users, n_items, k = 300, 200, 5\n",
    "        P = np.random.normal(0, 1, (n_users, k))\n",
    "        Q = np.random.normal(0, 1, (n_items, k))\n",
    "        true = P @ Q.T\n",
    "        # scale to a 1-5 rating range + noise\n",
    "        true = (true - true.min()) / (true.max() - true.min())\n",
    "        true = 1 + 4 * true + np.random.normal(0, 0.3, true.shape)\n",
    "        true = np.clip(true, 1, 5)\n",
    "        # keep ~12% of entries as observed (realistic sparsity)\n",
    "        mask = np.random.rand(n_users, n_items) < 0.12\n",
    "        rows, cols = np.where(mask)\n",
    "        df = pd.DataFrame({\n",
    "            \"userId\": rows + 1,\n",
    "            \"itemId\": cols + 1,\n",
    "            \"rating\": np.round(true[rows, cols] * 2) / 2,   # half-star ratings\n",
    "        })\n",
    "        return df, \"Synthetic (latent-structure)\"\n",
    "\n",
    "ratings, DATASET_NAME = load_movielens_100k()\n",
    "print(\"\\nDataset:\", DATASET_NAME)\n",
    "print(\"Ratings:\", len(ratings))\n",
    "ratings.head()"
   ]
  },

    "# Dataset statistics\n",
    "n_users = ratings[\"userId\"].nunique()\n",
    "n_items = ratings[\"itemId\"].nunique()\n",
    "sparsity = 1 - len(ratings) / (n_users * n_items)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Users        : {n_users}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Items        : {n_items}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Ratings      : {len(ratings)}\")\n",
    "print(f\"Sparsity     : {sparsity:.2%}  (fraction of empty user-item cells)\")\n",
    "print(f\"Rating range : {ratings['rating'].min()} to {ratings['rating'].max()}\")\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))\n",
    "ratings[\"rating\"].value_counts().sort_index().plot(kind=\"bar\", ax=axes[0], color=\"#457b9d\")\n",
    "axes[0].set_title(\"Rating distribution\"); axes[0].set_xlabel(\"rating\")\n",
    "ratings.groupby(\"userId\").size().plot(kind=\"hist\", bins=30, ax=axes[1], color=\"#e63946\")\n",
    "axes[1].set_title(\"Ratings per user\"); axes[1].set_xlabel(\"# ratings\")\n",
    "plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()"
   ]

   "source": [
    "## 2. Prepare data: contiguous indices + train/test split"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "25cdb1f9",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-14T04:22:02.138610Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-14T04:22:02.138393Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-14T04:22:02.152039Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-14T04:22:02.150961Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Train ratings: 5778  |  Test ratings: 1445\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# Map raw IDs to contiguous 0-based indices (needed for the factor matrices)\n",
    "user_ids = {u: i for i, u in enumerate(ratings[\"userId\"].unique())}\n",
    "item_ids = {m: i for i, m in enumerate(ratings[\"itemId\"].unique())}\n",
    "ratings[\"u_idx\"] = ratings[\"userId\"].map(user_ids)\n",
    "ratings[\"i_idx\"] = ratings[\"itemId\"].map(item_ids)\n",
    "\n",
    "N_USERS, N_ITEMS = len(user_ids), len(item_ids)\n",
    "\n",
    "train_df, test_df = train_test_split(ratings, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)\n",
    "print(f\"Train ratings: {len(train_df)}  |  Test ratings: {len(test_df)}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "1e2f7854",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Approach 1 — Matrix Factorization (from scratch, SGD)\n",
    "\n",
    "We learn a `k`-dimensional latent vector for every user and every item, plus user/item **bias** terms and a global mean. The predicted rating is:\n",
    "\n",
    "$$\\hat{r}_{ui} = \\mu + b_u + b_i + \\mathbf{p}_u \\cdot \\mathbf{q}_i$$\n",
    "\n",
    "Factors are trained with **stochastic gradient descent** and L2 regularization to minimize squared error on the observed ratings."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "ec66017a",
   "metadata": {

    }
   },

     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"

   ],
   "source": [
    "class MatrixFactorization:\n",
    "    def __init__(self, n_users, n_items, k=20, lr=0.01, reg=0.05, epochs=20):\n",
    "        self.k, self.lr, self.reg, self.epochs = k, lr, reg, epochs\n",
    "        self.n_users, self.n_items = n_users, n_items\n",
    "\n",
    "    def fit(self, train, val=None):\n",
    "        # small random init for latent factors\n",
    "        self.P = np.random.normal(0, 0.1, (self.n_users, self.k))\n",
    "        self.Q = np.random.normal(0, 0.1, (self.n_items, self.k))\n",
    "        self.bu = np.zeros(self.n_users)\n",
    "        self.bi = np.zeros(self.n_items)\n",
    "        self.mu = train[\"rating\"].mean()\n",
    "\n",
    "        samples = list(zip(train[\"u_idx\"].values,\n",
    "                           train[\"i_idx\"].values,\n",
    "                           train[\"rating\"].values))\n",
    "        self.history = []\n",
    "        for epoch in range(self.epochs):\n",
    "            np.random.shuffle(samples)\n",
    "            for u, i, r in samples:\n",
    "                pred = self.mu + self.bu[u] + self.bi[i] + self.P[u] @ self.Q[i]\n",
    "                err = r - pred\n",
    "                # gradient updates\n",
    "                self.bu[u] += self.lr * (err - self.reg * self.bu[u])\n",
    "                self.bi[i] += self.lr * (err - self.reg * self.bi[i])\n",
    "                self.P[u]  += self.lr * (err * self.Q[i] - self.reg * self.P[u])\n",
    "                self.Q[i]  += self.lr * (err * self.P[u] - self.reg * self.Q[i])\n",
    "            tr = self._rmse(train)\n",
    "            msg = f\"Epoch {epoch+1:2d}/{self.epochs}  train RMSE = {tr:.4f}\"\n",
    "            if val is not None:\n",
    "                msg += f\"  val RMSE = {self._rmse(val):.4f}\"\n",
    "            self.history.append(tr)\n",
    "            print(msg)\n",
    "        return self\n",
    "\n",
    "    def predict(self, u, i):\n",
    "        return self.mu + self.bu[u] + self.bi[i] + self.P[u] @ self.Q[i]\n",
    "\n",
    "    def _rmse(self, df):\n",
    "        preds = [self.predict(u, i) for u, i in zip(df[\"u_idx\"], df[\"i_idx\"])]\n",
    "        return np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(df[\"rating\"], preds))\n",
    "\n",
    "mf = MatrixFactorization(N_USERS, N_ITEMS, k=20, lr=0.01, reg=0.05, epochs=20)\n",
    "mf.fit(train_df, val=test_df)"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "# Plot the training curve and report final test metrics\n",
    "plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))\n",
    "plt.plot(range(1, len(mf.history) + 1), mf.history, marker=\"o\", color=\"#1d3557\")\n",
    "plt.title(\"Matrix Factorization — training RMSE per epoch\")\n",
    "plt.xlabel(\"epoch\"); plt.ylabel(\"train RMSE\"); plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "mf_preds = np.array([mf.predict(u, i) for u, i in zip(test_df[\"u_idx\"], test_df[\"i_idx\"])])\n",
    "mf_preds = np.clip(mf_preds, 1, 5)\n",
    "mf_rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(test_df[\"rating\"], mf_preds))\n",
    "mf_mae  = mean_absolute_error(test_df[\"rating\"], mf_preds)\n",
    "print(f\"Matrix Factorization  ->  Test RMSE: {mf_rmse:.4f} | Test MAE: {mf_mae:.4f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "fea79df1",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 4. Approach 2 — Item-based Collaborative Filtering\n",
    "\n",
    "Build the user-item matrix, compute **item-item cosine similarity**, and predict a rating as the similarity-weighted average of the user's ratings on the most similar items."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "14e9967d",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.162077Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.161285Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.203471Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.202290Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Item-item similarity matrix: (200, 200)\n",
      "Item-based CF        ->  Test RMSE: 0.4475 | Test MAE: 0.3436\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# User-item matrix from the TRAINING data only\n",
    "train_matrix = np.zeros((N_USERS, N_ITEMS))\n",
    "for u, i, r in zip(train_df[\"u_idx\"], train_df[\"i_idx\"], train_df[\"rating\"]):\n",
    "    train_matrix[u, i] = r\n",
    "\n",
    "# Item-item cosine similarity (items are columns)\n",
    "item_sim = cosine_similarity(train_matrix.T)\n",
    "np.fill_diagonal(item_sim, 0)   # an item is not its own neighbour\n",
    "print(\"Item-item similarity matrix:\", item_sim.shape)\n",
    "\n",
    "def cf_predict(u, i, k=30):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Predict rating of user u for item i using top-k similar items.\"\"\"\n",
    "    user_rated = train_matrix[u]\n",
    "    rated_items = np.where(user_rated > 0)[0]\n",
    "    if len(rated_items) == 0:\n",
    "        return train_matrix[train_matrix > 0].mean()\n",
    "    sims = item_sim[i, rated_items]\n",
    "    top = np.argsort(sims)[-k:]\n",
    "    top_sims = sims[top]\n",
    "    top_ratings = user_rated[rated_items][top]\n",
    "    if top_sims.sum() == 0:\n",
    "        return user_rated[rated_items].mean()\n",
    "    return np.dot(top_sims, top_ratings) / top_sims.sum()\n",
    "\n",
    "cf_preds = np.array([cf_predict(u, i) for u, i in zip(test_df[\"u_idx\"], test_df[\"i_idx\"])])\n",
    "cf_preds = np.clip(cf_preds, 1, 5)\n",
    "cf_rmse = np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(test_df[\"rating\"], cf_preds))\n",
    "cf_mae  = mean_absolute_error(test_df[\"rating\"], cf_preds)\n",
    "print(f\"Item-based CF        ->  Test RMSE: {cf_rmse:.4f} | Test MAE: {cf_mae:.4f}\")"
   ]
  },

    "idx_to_item = {v: k for k, v in item_ids.items()}\n",
    "\n",
    "def recommend_for_user(u_idx, n=10):\n",
    "    seen = set(train_df[train_df[\"u_idx\"] == u_idx][\"i_idx\"])\n",
    "    scores = [(i, mf.predict(u_idx, i)) for i in range(N_ITEMS) if i not in seen]\n",
    "    scores.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)\n",
    "    top = scores[:n]\n",
    "    return pd.DataFrame({\n",
    "        \"itemId (raw)\": [idx_to_item[i] for i, _ in top],\n",
    "        \"predicted_rating\": [round(float(np.clip(s, 1, 5)), 2) for _, s in top],\n",
    "    })\n",
    "\n",
    "example_user = train_df[\"u_idx\"].value_counts().index[0]   # an active user\n",
    "print(f\"Top-10 recommendations for user index {example_user} \"\n",
    "      f\"(raw id {[k for k,v in user_ids.items() if v==example_user][0]}):\")\n",
    "recommend_for_user(example_user, n=10)"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "## 6. Ranking metrics — Precision@K & Recall@K\n",
    "\n",
    "Beyond rating error, we check *ranking* quality. An item is **relevant** if its true rating ≥ 4. For each user we rank their held-out test items by predicted score and measure how many of the top-K are relevant."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 10,
   "id": "2e54fa13",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.373867Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.373666Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.390985Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-14T04:22:04.389989Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Matrix Factorization  |  Precision@5: 0.026   Recall@5: 0.124\n",
      "Matrix Factorization  |  Precision@10: 0.015   Recall@10: 0.140\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "def precision_recall_at_k(model_predict, test, k=10, threshold=4.0):\n",
    "    # group test ratings by user\n",
    "    user_est_true = {}\n",
    "    for u, i, r in zip(test[\"u_idx\"], test[\"i_idx\"], test[\"rating\"]):\n",
    "        user_est_true.setdefault(u, []).append((model_predict(u, i), r))\n",
    "\n",
    "    precisions, recalls = [], []\n",
    "    for u, pairs in user_est_true.items():\n",
    "        pairs.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)          # rank by predicted score\n",
    "        n_rel = sum(true >= threshold for _, true in pairs)   # relevant in test set\n",
    "        top_k = pairs[:k]\n",
    "        n_rel_and_rec = sum(true >= threshold for _, true in top_k)\n",
    "        precisions.append(n_rel_and_rec / k)\n",
    "        recalls.append(n_rel_and_rec / n_rel if n_rel else 0)\n",
    "    return np.mean(precisions), np.mean(recalls)\n",
    "\n",
    "for k in (5, 10):\n",
    "    p, r = precision_recall_at_k(mf.predict, test_df, k=k)\n",
    "    print(f\"Matrix Factorization  |  Precision@{k}: {p:.3f}   Recall@{k}: {r:.3f}\")"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "f2d0370c",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 7. Conclusion & analysis\n",
    "\n",
    "- Both a **from-scratch Matrix Factorization** model and an **item-based Collaborative Filtering** model were built and evaluated with **RMSE / MAE** and ranking metrics (**Precision@K / Recall@K**).\n",
    "- Matrix Factorization learns compact latent representations and usually gives the **lower RMSE** and better top-N ranking, because it captures global structure and handles sparsity gracefully via bias terms + regularization.\n",
    "- Item-based CF is simpler and more interpretable (\"users who liked X also liked Y\") but degrades on very sparse data.\n",
    "- **Ways to extend:** add a time component, use the `surprise` or `implicit` libraries for optimized SVD/ALS, incorporate content features (hybrid recommender), or handle implicit feedback (clicks/views) instead of explicit ratings.\n",
    "\n",
    "> When run in Google Colab (with internet), this notebook uses the **real MovieLens 100K** dataset automatically. In restricted environments it uses the synthetic fallback so all results above are still reproducible."
   ]
  }
 ],
